file_insights.changes_made: Added three new model classes: AllergenSeverity (TextChoices), Allergen, ProductAllergen, and NutritionInfo
key_additions: AllergenSeverity enum with CONTAINS, MAY_CONTAIN, TRACES options
Allergen model with platform-level definitions (UUID PK, slug, code, icon, description, sort_order, is_active)
ProductAllergen junction table (links Product to Allergen with severity levels)
NutritionInfo one-to-one model with comprehensive nutritional fields (macros, micros, custom JSON)
architectural_patterns: Platform-level vs tenant-scoped models (Allergen is platform-wide, ProductAllergen is tenant-accessible via Product FK)
SoftDeleteManager pattern consistently applied across all new models
TimeStampedMixin and SoftDeleteMixin mixins for audit trails
UUID primary keys for global uniqueness
Unique constraints (allergen code/slug globally unique, product-allergen pair unique)
Comprehensive database indexing on foreign keys and filtered fields

patterns_discovered.description: Allergen model is platform-level (no organization FK) while ProductAllergen bridges to organization context via Product FK. This allows consistent allergen definitions across all tenants.
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces data duplication and ensures allergen consistency across organizations

patterns_discovered.description: ProductAllergen not only links Product to Allergen but adds severity level and optional notes fields
patterns_discovered.benefit: Captures contextual information about allergen relationships without polluting main models

patterns_discovered.description: Every field includes verbose_name, help_text, and detailed docstrings with usage examples
patterns_discovered.benefit: Improves developer experience and API documentation generation

patterns_discovered.description: Both SoftDeleteManager (excludes soft-deleted) and all_objects Manager (includes all) available on soft-deletable models
patterns_discovered.benefit: Provides flexibility for queries that need to see deleted records vs only active records

patterns_discovered.description: severity_display property on ProductAllergen and is_available property on Allergen
patterns_discovered.benefit: Encapsulates display logic and allows template-friendly access to enum choices

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Allergen has no organization FK - platform-level model
gotchas_discovered.impact: Queries for allergens should NOT include organization_id filter, unlike most other menu models
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documentation explicitly notes this exception in docstring and class Meta comments

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: ProductAllergen uses on_delete=CASCADE for both FKs
gotchas_discovered.impact: Deleting a product or allergen will cascade-delete the relationship. May need soft-delete strategy if hard deletes are avoided elsewhere.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: SoftDeleteMixin on ProductAllergen provides soft-delete capability despite CASCADE setting

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: NutritionInfo has many optional fields (blank=True, null=True)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Need validation logic to ensure at least some nutritional data is provided, otherwise schema conflicts with intent
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: None applied in current implementation - could add clean() method validation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Unique constraint on (product, allergen) in ProductAllergen
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot have duplicate allergen entries for same product - good for data integrity but could silently fail on create() if not handled
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should use get_or_create() or explicit checking in service layer

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: DecimalField with max_digits=8, decimal_places=2 for nutrition values
gotchas_discovered.impact: Limits to 6 integer digits (99.99 max for most nutrients) - may be insufficient for some values like sodium (can exceed 1000mg)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: No validation applied; some fields should use larger max_digits

key_achievements: Successfully created platform-level Allergen model with proper UUID PKs and indexing
Implemented ProductAllergen junction table with severity levels and tenant isolation
Created comprehensive NutritionInfo one-to-one model with standard + custom nutrients
Added extensive documentation with usage examples in docstrings
approach_outcome.testing_status: Not evident in commit - only model definition provided
approach_outcome.documentation_status: Excellent - detailed docstrings with usage examples and critical rules

recommendations.recommendation: Add clean() method to NutritionInfo to validate that at least one nutritional field is provided
recommendations.rationale: Empty nutrition records provide no value and indicate incomplete data

recommendations.recommendation: Increase max_digits on nutrition DecimalFields from 8 to 10+ (e.g., sodium can exceed 1000mg)
recommendations.rationale: Current constraints may truncate valid nutritional values

recommendations.recommendation: Document and provide query helpers for common operations: 'get allergens for product', 'products containing allergen', 'nutrition summary'
recommendations.rationale: Prevents misuse of platform vs tenant scoping rules

recommendations.recommendation: Add comprehensive tests for unique_together constraint on ProductAllergen(product, allergen)
recommendations.rationale: Ensures duplicate prevention works as expected and tests error handling

recommendations.recommendation: Create Django management command to seed standard allergens (14 major allergens) at app initialization
recommendations.rationale: Allergen docstring mentions this but no implementation is evident

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding select_related() hints to ProductAllergen.Meta documentation for common queries
recommendations.rationale: Reduces N+1 queries when fetching product allergens with allergen details
